quickAnswer: To handle turning one long video into short vertical clips, start with long videos, decide that the output must be short clips, and use AI only after the source material is clear. Start with long videos, decide that the output must be short clips, use OpusClip for one reviewable version, and check it before changing formats or adding another tool. OpusClip should create the first reviewable version, CapCut belongs in the second pass only when short clips needs more control, and Descript should be saved for review or export after the core result is right.
quickDecision: For turning one long video into short vertical clips, choose OpusClip first when the input is long videos and the output needs to be short clips. Choose CapCut when short matters more. Choose Descript when you need a lighter review, export, or fallback pass.

steps.title: Prepare long videos
steps.detail: Put the real input in one place before opening a tool: long videos, webinar recordings, ad scripts, talking-head footage, turning one long video into short vertical clips, any required facts, the audience, and the format you need at the end. Remove private details you are not allowed to share.
steps.why: AI output is only as useful as the material and constraints you give it.
steps.output: A clean input bundle and one clear target format: short clips.

steps.title: Ask for the first usable version
steps.detail: Use OpusClip for a first pass that tries to turn one long video into short vertical clips. Ask it to preserve source facts, flag uncertainty, and organize the result into sections you can review.
steps.why: The first pass should create a reviewable short clips, not a finished answer you trust blindly.
steps.output: A structured short clips with visible gaps or uncertain points.

steps.title: Check the answer before polishing
steps.detail: Compare the AI output with long videos. Fix names, dates, numbers, unsupported claims, missing context, and tone before you ask for nicer wording.
steps.why: Polished wrong information is harder to catch than rough information.
steps.output: A corrected short clips that is ready for formatting or wording cleanup.

steps.title: Change the format only after the facts are right
steps.detail: Use CapCut when the verified short clips needs a different format, structure, or source-aware second pass. Keep the same facts from long videos so the rewrite does not drift from the original.
steps.why: Format changes are safer after the substance is already checked.
steps.output: A version that fits the channel, document, post, or recipient.

steps.title: Finish with a human review
steps.detail: Use Descript for cleanup only after the main short clips is checked against long videos. Read the final result as the recipient would: on the device, in the app, or in the document where it will actually be used.
steps.why: The final review catches tone, missing context, and practical usability issues.
steps.output: A final result you can send, post, study from, or save with confidence.

faqs.question: Which tool should I start with for short clips?
